path: Problem: Find a record by key in a lookup table and return the difference between two fields from the matched row
Action: Use VLOOKUP to retrieve two different columns from the same matched row, then subtract; wrap with IF/AND and IFERROR to suppress missing/invalid results

Observation: This works when the lookup key is in the first column of the lookup range and the desired return columns are to the right; COUNTIF (or IFERROR around VLOOKUP) can be used to avoid errors when the key is absent
Problem: Explain array constants used to select multiple columns and apply signs when combining multiple looked-up values
Action: Use SUMPRODUCT with VLOOKUP returning multiple columns via an array constant and multiply by a sign array constant

Observation: An array constant like {i,j} makes VLOOKUP return multiple column values at once; a sign array like {-1,1} applies subtraction/addition to those returned values so SUMPRODUCT can combine them into a single result
